summary: Recent updates to the video capture functionality in Firefox have focused on improving code quality by complying with modern coding standards. Specifically, the implementation has adopted the use of `nullptr` instead of traditional null values, following clang-tidy’s guidelines. This change enhances the robustness of the codebase, making it easier to maintain while ensuring compatibility with contemporary C++ development practices. While users may not see immediate visible changes, this type of improvement plays a vital role in maintaining a reliable and effective video capture experience.
